reasoning: The FRED statistical analysis computed a short-term probability of increase of 0.71 based on recent positive linear trends and seasonal priors. I used this as my primary probability anchor for the shortest horizon (April 19), slightly discounting it to 0.67 due to recent momentum and geopolitical developments (Iran conflict) driving mild flight-to-safety behavior which typically pushes yields tracking down. For later dates, macro indicators suggest an easing Federal Funds rate into 2027 (shifting from ~3.6% down towards 3.0%), which should broadly reduce real yields. To reflect increasing uncertainty over extended horizons, I progressively fade the probabilities toward the 0.5 base-rate of a random walk.
